key_insight: A multimodal pretreatment signature combining clinical, blood, tumor, and molecular data predicted primary resistance to anti-PD-(L)1 therapy in advanced NSCLC better than PD-L1 or TMB alone.
clinical_relevance: This study suggests that resistance risk may be stratified more accurately with integrated biomarker profiling, potentially informing first-line and later-line immunotherapy decisions in advanced NSCLC.
method: Prospective multicenter PIONeeR study across 17 centers in advanced NSCLC
Six-layer pretreatment profiling: clinical, routine labs, circulating immune markers, soluble vascular markers, digital pathology, transcriptomics, and genomics
Benchmarking of 36 feature-selection methods and 10 machine-learning models using .632 optimism correction
evidence_signal: In 435 evaluable patients, the final multimodal model achieved an optimism-corrected AUROC of 0.73 ± 0.05 and PPV of 0.63 ± 0.07 overall, outperforming PD-L1 and TMB.
watch_item: The signature is complex and setting-dependent, so external validation and real-world implementation feasibility will be essential before routine use.
paper_title: Multimodal profiling for prediction of primary resistance to anti-PD-(L)1 therapy in advanced non-small-cell lung cancer: the prospective PIONeeR biomarkers study
